mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp, or the signal shows no statistical edge over random baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and weakening economic momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the regime filter eliminates all predictive signal.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with stronger corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in improved fundamentals.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 levels.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level exceeding 305 indicates entrenched inflation that forces aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in >58% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 305, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in >65% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates persistent supply chain bottlenecks that disrupt earnings momentum, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions in recovery regimes produce positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when regime filters are applied.
